violations.text: Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the bar. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/05/2026

Food items prepared in advance were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2026

The cutting boards in the kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 01/12/2026
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on the prep units on the cooking line. Gaskets must be maintained in good condition. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-Food contact surfaces must be maintained. New Violation. Correct By: 01/12/2026
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2026
Mop bucket must be emptied after use and mops must be allowed to air dry after use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/05/2026
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by the mop and bucket. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/10/2024

Soap and paper towel was not available at the hand-washing sink in the bar. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap, and paper towel. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/10/2024

Food items in the walk in cooler prepared in advanced was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/10/2024
Food items were observed stored on the floor in the kitchen and in the freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/10/2024

Knives were observed stored on an unclean surface (between prep units) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/10/2024
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on prep unit one, prep unit two, and the walk in cooler. Gaskets must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2024

The cutting boards in the kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the walk in cooler blower fans have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non food contact surfaces must be maintained. New Violation. Correct By: 09/14/2024

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024
